_comment: RECOMMENDED MCP SERVERS — this file is NOT loaded automatically.
The be plugin deliberately ships no auto-started MCP servers: every server
adds processes, permissions, and tool-schema tokens to your context.
How to use: review each entry, keep only what the project needs, and copy
the result to your project's .mcp.json (project root).
- Keep under ~10 servers enabled — tool schemas dominate context cost
- PIN versions for supply-chain safety: replace `npx -y pkg` with
`npx -y pkg@<reviewed-version>`. Unpinned npx runs newly published code
on every start. Each server below notes its data boundary.
- Per-server `_comment` states what data leaves your machine. Prefer
read-only / local servers; never point write-capable servers at prod.

github._comment: Data boundary: sends repo/PR/issue data to GitHub's API using your PAT. Pin a version. Used by proc-code-review and the sprint protocol.

context7._comment: Data boundary: sends library names + your query to Upstash Context7 to fetch up-to-date docs. No repo code leaves the machine. Pairs with proc-dependency-management.

sequential-thinking._comment: Data boundary: local only — structured chain-of-thought tool, no egress. Useful for complex planning/debugging.

postgres._comment: Data boundary: connects to the DB you point it at and can read rows. READ-ONLY inspection — point at a DEV database, NEVER production. Pairs with be-db-migrations and dev-data-analyst.

playwright._comment: Data boundary: drives a local browser to the URLs you give it (can reach intranet/localhost). Used for E2E/browser checks alongside qa-engineer. Disable when not testing UI.
